text: This is a video of a patient that's undergoing a cholecystectomy after a previous sleeve gastrectomy. Here you can see the sleeve gastrectomy. That's actually very healthy in appearance.
The surgeon lifts the gallbladder, identifying the cystic duct and cystic artery as the two main structures entering the gallbladder.
The surgeon clips the cystic artery on the right side of the screen while the cystic duct, which carries bile, is visible on the left side.
The robotic platform provides a clear view of the anatomy as the surgeon applies clips to seal the cystic duct and artery in preparation for cutting.

The surgeon cuts the cystic artery, which supplies blood to the gallbladder, after identifying the anatomy clearly. An additional clip is placed over the cystic duct, which carries bile from the liver to the gallbladder, before cutting it.
The surgeon uses robotic scissors to separate the gallbladder from the liver bed, with clear visualization aiding in precise dissection.
The surgeon applies traction on the gallbladder, revealing the space needed to separate it from the liver. The robotic instruments facilitate this dissection with minimal blood loss.

So again, it's important to realize that all this surgery is done through three eight millimeter instruments, including a camera. And again, we have a three D high definition camera that gives us a very clear image and a clear picture. So we can really see what we're doing to the patient and we're harming as little tissue as possible.

The surgeon removes the gallbladder from the liver bed and places it aside. The gallbladder is then extracted through a small incision at the belly button, which is subsequently closed with sutures to prevent hernia formation.

The patient did very well and was discharged home the same day. So again, this is another example of why I like using these robotic tools to help our patients. I think the anatomy is clearly seen. The patients tend to do very well after surgery.
The surgeon uses suction to remove residual air from the patient's abdomen, revealing the final surgical result with the gallbladder successfully removed.
